name: Kahlua
directions: Combine the sugar and 4 cups water.
Boil for 10 minutes.
Set aside.
Combine coffee and 2 cups water.
Boil for 8 minutes.
Add to sugar water.
Let sit until room temperature.
Add vodka and vanilla to coffee mixture.
Refrigerate.

name: Orange-Lime Margaritas
directions: In a small heavy saucepan, combine the water and sugar.
Cook over medium heat until it forms a clear syrup, about 10 minutes.
Set aside and allow the syrup to cool completely.
Fill a large pitcher half full with chipped ice; stir in the lime juice, orange juice, sugar syrup, tequila, and orange liqueur.
Cut the lime into 8 wedges.
Using a wedge of lime, rub the rims of four glasses with the lime and invert the glasses into a plate of coarse salt.
Fill each glass with chipped ice and pour the margarita mix, evenly divided, over the ice.
Garnish each drink with a wedge of lime.

name: Long Island Iced Tea
directions: Stir over ice.

similar.name: Long Island Iced Tea (Applebee's/TGI Friday's style)

name: Cold Coffee (without sugar)
directions: Put the instant coffee powder in a coffee mug/cup and add the water to it.
Now carefully blend the coffee with the water to form a rather fluid paste.
Once that is done, whip the paste vigourously for a few minutes with a spoon until tiny bubbles form on the surface.
Now add the cold milk to this paste and stir slowly until the coffee paste has completely blended with the milk.
A nice, thin layer of foam will form automatically on the surface, and your coffee is ready to drink!
Note: This cold coffee can also be made adding sugar or an artificial sweetener.
Add the sugar/sweetener along with the coffee powder (at the beginning) and an additional teaspoon of water to make sure that it all blends together.
You might have to whip the mixture for a little while longer to make sure that you have a perfectly smooth blend.

name: Garden Veggie Juice
directions: Peel, core, seed and chop the tomatoes.
Place the tomatoes, green pepper, and the cucumber in electric blender and blend at high speed for 1 minute or until smooth.
Add the lemon juice, horseradish, red pepper sauce, dill, and ice cubes; blend 1 minute longer, or until smooth.

name: Tropical Margarita
directions: Mix first 4 ingredients in glass measuring cup.
Fill 2 margarita glasses with ice.
Pour margarita mixture over.
Garnish with lime slices and orange peel twists.

name: Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients in a food processor or blender.
Store in tightly covered container.
To serve combine 3 (or more) tablespoons of mix with 1 cup boiling water; stir to blend.
For added fun mix in mini-marshmallows.

similar.name: Hot Cocoa Mix in a Mug

name: Mocha Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients in a food processor or blender.
Store in tightly covered container.
To serve combine 3 tablespoons of mix with 1 cup boiling water; stir to blend.

name: Sleepy Time Mocha Coffee Mix
directions: Blend sugar and cocoa until all lumps are gone.
Stir in the rest of the ingredients.
Store in an air tight container.

name: Spiced Cocoa
directions: In a medium sized bowl, combine all ingredients.
Mix well.
Put in resealable container.
To make cocoa, use 1-2 tablespoons mix per cup of hot milk.
Serve hot or iced.

name: Flavored Coffee Creamers
directions: Combine all ingredients.
Blend well.
Add to taste in hot coffee.

name: Creamy Orange Liqueur
directions: In a saucepan, bring sugar and water to a boil; let cool.
Whisk vodka, condensed milk, orange juice concentrate and vanilla into sugar-water mixture until creamy and well-combined.
Pour into attractive bottles, seal, and keep stored in refrigerator.
Makes approximately 5 cups; keeps well in the fridge for at least 2 weeks.

name: Vegan Eggnog
directions: In blender or food processor, combine all ingredients except nutmeg; blend thoroughly, stopping occasionally to scrape down sides of bowl.
Serve well-chilled and dusted with nutmeg.

name: Chocolat Hot Chocolate
directions: Melt together chocolate and cream in double boiler.
Warm through until desired temp, I usually don't like it really hot.
Pour into 4 mugs.
Top with whipped cream and a sprinkle of cinnamon.

name: Yum Kahlua
directions: Boil the water, add sugar and stir until it dissolves.
Turn down heat to simmer and add coffee, stir until it dissolves.
Simmer for 10 minutes stirring occasionally.
Cool completely (if in a hurry, stick it in the fridge).
When completely cooled, add vanilla then alcohol and stir together.

name: Laung Chai (Cloves Tea)
directions: Put water in a pan on high heat.
Crush cloves and add to the water.
Wait till water starts boiling.
Now lower the heat and let it simmer for 2 minutes.
Add tea leaves/ tea bag and let it boil for a few seconds on high heat again.
Add milk and give one boil.
Add sugar.
Switch off the gas and cover the lid tightly.
Let it stand for a minute and sip sip sip!
Adjust milk and sugar as per your preferences!

similar.name: Better Chai from a Chai Tea Bag

name: Nuts and Holly
directions: Place all ingreedients into a shaker cup filled with ice.
Shake the cup and then strain into 2 shot glasses.
Drink up!

name: Fruity Tooty
directions: Mix the Banana and Watermelon liqueur's in a shot glass (which means you don't have to try to layer them once you pour them give them a little stir).
Top with Vodka.
Time to enjoy the drink!

name: Big Red Monster
directions: Pour all the ingredients into a shot glass.
1,2,3 down.

name: P.d.c.
directions: Layer into a shot glass in this order Creme de Menthe, Sambuca, Chatreuse and then Bailey's.
Enjoy the taste!

name: Nuts and Berries
directions: Pour Vodka and then Amaretto into a shot glass.
Then add bailey's to the top.
Time to drink, so down the hatch.

name: Post-Modern Cocktail
directions: Place all ingreedients into a shaker glass filled with ice and shake.
Strain into a nice fancy cocktail glass.

name: Barmans Nightmare
directions: Layer the ingredients in the order on the list of ingredients.
Look. Admire your pretty drink (If you got it right).
Drink.

name: Long Island Iced Tea (Applebee's/TGI Friday's style)
directions: Add crushed ice to pint glass.
Add first 4 ingredients.
Shake.
Top with cola and sour mix.
Serve.
*You can add raspberry liquor to this, and call it a Black Opal, even more tasty!

name: Gingerbread Man (Drink)
directions: Place all ingredients in a shaker glass with ice.
Shake.
Pour into 3 shot glasses.
1,2,3 drink.

name: Irish Nut
directions: Pour Bailey's in a shot glass and then pour in the Amaretto.
Drink.

name: Purple Fantasy
directions: Place the vodka and rum in the bottom of a highball glass with ice.
Pour in cordial and fill up with lemonade.
Drink.

name: Let's Tango
directions: Layer in a shot glass in this order; rum, Sambuca, triple sec.
Admire a fantastic drink.
Drink your fantastic drink.

name: Shot in the Grass
directions: Layer the ingredients.
Admire.
Skull.

name: B-52
directions: Place ice in the bottom of a lowball glass.
Try to layer the ingredients in the order above.
If you get it right congratulate yourself.
Now enjoy yourself and drink.

name: Jamaican YoYo
directions: Pour ingredients into a shot glass in the order given.
I have found that i have real trouble layering them.
Enjoy the drink.

name: Coffee Fantasy
directions: Place all in a normal glass.
Enjoy the fantasy.

name: Californian Lemonade
directions: Place all ingredients into a shaker cup filled with ice.
Shake.
Strain into 4 shooter glasses.
1,2,3 shoot.

name: Almond Delight
directions: Pour both ingredients into a lowball glass over ice.
Serve.
Drink.

name: Tahitian Coffee
directions: Combine the first 3 ingredients in a pitcher, stirring until sugar dissolves.
Pour into mugs, and top with ice cream (2 tablespoons per mug).
Garnish with cinnamon sticks, if desired.

name: Tequila Shooter
directions: Pour the tequila into a shot glass.
First skull the tequila and then suck the pepper.
Sit their and feel the heat.
Don't be tempted to drink.

name: Tequila Slammer
directions: Pour the tequila into a shooter glass.
Make a wet patch on your wrist and pour some salt on it.
Now first you lick the salt.
Then you sip (I really mean skull) the tequila.
Then you suck the lemon.

name: Red Russian
directions: Fill an Old Fashioned glass with ice.
Add ingredients and stir.

name: Frozen Brandy Alexander
directions: Add all ingredients to a blender and blend until smooth.
Pour into and Old Fashioned glass.
Sprinkle with nutmeg.

name: Frappe Cappuccino Copycat
directions: Combine all of the ingredients in a pitcher or covered container.
Stir or shake until sugar is dissolved.
Chill and serve cold.
To make the mocha variety, add a pinch of cocoa powder to the mixture before combining.

name: Hot Cinnamon Coffee
directions: Mix the milk and water together in a pot and heat on a low flame.
As the mixture just starts to boil, add the coffee powder and stir.
Bring to a boil and switch off burner.
Add cinnamon, stir, and pour into two small cups to serve.

name: Vallie's Rum & Pepsi
directions: Mix in a glass, your rum, then your VERY cold pepsi.

(put it in the freezer for an hour if you're worried about how cold, but NOT the whole can, okay?) Garnish with your cute lil black stir straw, then add your tiki umbrella, and you have one POWERFUL tiki rumnpep!
Special warning: If you don't drink very often, don't drink this, or you'll regret you ever did.
Only the strong and highly tolerant can handle this one.
For garnish: 1 cute lil umbrella garnish, 1 black stir straw.

name: Double-O B
directions: Ice to fill your glass.
garnish with orange peel (there's a reason to squeeze them yourself).
Fill your glass with ice.
add bacardi O.
add strained, hand-squeezed orange juice.
stir.
garnish.
drink.
enjoy.

name: Black Irish Frappe
directions: Mix ingredients in a blender and then add ice.
Blend until smooth.

name: The Toronto Star's Irish Coffee
directions: In a pre-warmed stemmed heatproof glass (yes, you can also use a prewarmed coffee mug, but it doesn't look as good), pour in whisky, then add sugar, then pour in coffee up to within 1/2-inch of rim.
Stir well to dissolve sugar.
Fill to the brim with whipped cream.
Serve, enjoy, relax!

name: Easy Ginger Tea
directions: Place grated ginger into a 2-cup glass measuring cup.
Fill to the 1-1/2 cup line with boiling water.
Set timer for 10 minutes.
When timer goes off, strain into a large mug and stir in sugar (you may need more or less, depending on how sweet you want your tea).
Taste; if it's too strong (too gingery) for you, add a little more boiling water to your mug, but I found the strong taste really grew on me, so I hope you try it this way first!
Sip and enjoy!
Note#1: If that's too much, you can use just 1 tsp grated ginger and just 1 cup of boiling water, but I like a big mug.
Note#2: a squeeze of lemon can be added, and maple syrup is an interesting replacement for the white sugar.
Note#3: If you triple or quadruple this recipe, you can make a lovely pot of ginger tea instead of a single serving.

name: White Hot Chocolate (using milk)
directions: Put about half of milk in a small pot.
Add other ingredients and stir often.
Bring almost to a boil.
Chocolate should be melted.
Add remaining milk to pan, and stir for about 30 seconds.
Remove from heat.
Serve with a dusting of cinnamon and a dusting of fine dark chocolate shavings.

name: Hot Cocoa for One
directions: Add sugar, cocoa and salt into a large cup.
Heat milk in microwave on high for 1 minute or until hot.
Pour milk into cocoa mixture; stir.
Stir in vanilla.

name: Quick Hot Cocoa
directions: Add dry milk, sugar, and cocoa in a mug.
Stir in water.
Top with whipped cream.

name: Rum Runner Martini
directions: Shake all ingredients together in cocktail shaker.
Pour into glass and garnish with speared orange slice and a cherry, if desired.

name: Quick Cocoa Mexicana for One
directions: Combine dry milk, sugar, cinnamon, and cocoa in mug or cup; stir in boiling water.
Stir in vanilla extract, inhale deeply, and indulge immediately!

name: Cafe Au Lait
directions: In a small saucepan over medium-low heat, heat the milk, sugar and cinnamon mixture until it simmers.
Place the coffee granules in a mug; add the hot milk and stir until blended.

similar.name: Low-Cal Iced Coffee With Milk (Cafe Au Lait)

name: IRISH Coffee
directions: Mix coffee, orange juice and lemon juice.
Pour into Irish whiskey glass.
Top with whipped cream.
Sippppppppppp!

name: Cocoa Mocha Coffee
directions: Mix all ingredients with boiling water in your favorite mug.
Adjust ingredients more or less to suit your tastes.
Enjoy!

name: Tequila Sunrise
directions: Place Ice in a tall glass.
Place remaining ingredients in a shaker, shake away and pour over ice OR withhold grenadine, shake away, pour over ice and slowly pour in grenadine so it swirls on the bottom of glass.

name: Emerald City "Martini"
directions: Mix it all together.
Shake with ice or serve with ice in- your preference.

name: Chilly Mochaccino
directions: Combine coffee and boiling water in blender.
Blend until dissolved.
Pour in chocolate milk and ice cubes.
Blend until smooth.
Pour into tall glasses.

name: Balsamic Bloody Mary
directions: In a large pitcher, combine the vegetable cocktail, celery seed, pepper, citrus juices, 2 Tbs grated horseradish, Worcestershire sauce, and hot pepper sauce and stir well to combine.
Cover and refrigerate for 1 hour.
Fill 4 glasses with ice.
Add 2 oz vodka to each and fill three-quarters full with the vegetable cocktail mix.
Sprinkle with the remaining horseradish, place 1 celery stalk in each glass, and squeeze a lime wedge over each glass before dropping the wedge into the glass.
Float a tsp of balsamic vinegar on the top of each drink and serve.

name: Spiced Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: Combine milk powder, cocoa powder, creamer and cinnamon.
Mix thoroughly.
Store in an airtight container.
Makes 8 1/2 cups of mix.
Before serving: For one serving, combine 1/3 cup mix with cup boiling water in a cup or mug.
Stir to dissolve mixture.
ENJOY!

name: Tropical Temper
directions: Blend all ingredients together, pour over ice and garnish with lime wheel.

name: Iced Chocolate
directions: Mix the cocoa powder to a smooth paste with some of the milk.
Place the rest of the milk on the stove in a pan.
Bring almost to a boil.
Add the cocoa paste and the sugar.
Stir till boiling point is reached.
Remove from heat.
Fill glasses up to 1/3rd with chipped ice.
Strain the cocoa onto this.
Add a heaped tsp. of whipped cream and serve immediately.

name: Sinfully delicious Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: This makes 1 gallon dry mix.
Mix all ingredients together and store sealed.
When you are ready for this sinful drink just fill a cup half full of mix and top off with boiling water.
Its out of this world.

similar.name: Creamy Hot Chocolate Mix in a Jar (For Gift-Giving)

name: French Hot Chocolate
directions: About 50 minutes before serving: In double boiler over hot, not boiling water (or in heavy 1 quart saucepan over low heat), melt chocolate and corn syrup until chocolate is melted and mixture is smooth, stirring occasionally with rubber spatula.
Cover and refrigerate 30 minutes or until mixture is cool.
Stir in vanilla.
In 2 quart saucepan over medium-low heat, heat milk until very hot and small bubbles form around edge (do not boil).
Meanwhile, in small bowl with mixer at medium-low speed, beat cream and chocolate mixture until soft peaks form.
To serve, pour milk into warm coffee pot.
Spoon whipped cream mixture into 8 coffee cups and fill with hot milk.

name: Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Simply stir together and store.
If desired, the mixture can be put in the blender for a few minutes.
This won't change the taste any, but will make it look prettier.
To serve, add about 6 tablespoons mix to 3/4 cup hot water, or to taste.

name: Brandied White Hot Chocolate
directions: In a heavy saucepan, heat milk and cream over medium-low heat for five minutes.
Add chocolate chunks to hot milk mixture, then stir in sugar and vanilla; keep stirring, gently, until chocolate melts.
Remove from heat and stir in brandy.
Serve in prewarmed mugs, garnished with a cinnamon stick or candy cane, if you wish.

name: Frappuccino
directions: mix all ingredients together.
blend on high for one minute to make it creamy.
enjoy! (add more coffee if you like. I add 1/8-1/4 cup more).

similar.name: Frappuccino, the Way I Do It (Starbucks Copycat!)

name: Wasabi Bloody Mary
directions: Combine lime juice and wasabi with a whisk, until wasabi dissolves.
Combine wasabi mixture, veggie juice, Worcestershire sauce, pepper sauce and salt in a pitcher; chill.
Stir in vodka and serve over ice.

name: Cappuccino Mix
directions: Toss all ingredients in a plastic bag and seal OR layer all 6 ingredients, in order given, in jar with tight fitting lid.
Directions for cappuccino:
Stir contents of jar before measuring.
Measure 2 tbsp mix into blender.
Add 1 cup boiling water.
Process until foamy.
Pour into mug.
Sprinkle with nutmeg.

name: Irish Cream
directions: Put first 7 ingredients into blender.
Blend until smooth.
Add whiskey.
Mix and adjust to taste (I usually add extra milk).
Pour into jar with tight-fitting lid.
Store in fridge for up to three weeks.

similar.name: Bailey's Irish Cream Liqueur (Gift-Giving or for Yourself!)

name: Curiously Good Peppermint Tea
directions: Boil the water (or microwave it for approximately 1 minute and 20 seconds).
Put in the Altoid and the tea bag.
Let the tea steep for about 1 minutes and 30 seconds-2 minutes.
Stir and make sure the Altoid has dissolved.
Add sugar if desired (although it is quite good without it!) and enjoy!

name: High Protein Hot Chocolate
directions: Shake everything up in a 1 gal zip lock bag.
Put two rounded tablespoons in a mug.
Add just enough water to make a thin smooth paste, stir until smooth.
Add a dash of vanilla extract if desired.
Fill mug with hot water, (boiling not necessary).
Stir and enjoy.

name: Bailey's (Copycat) Irish Cream
directions: Dissolve coffee in 1 oz very hot water.
Combine all ingredients in blender.
Blend& pour into container.
Store in refrigerator.

name: Honey Raspberry Iced Tea
directions: Add all ingredients to a large, heat-proof pitcher.
Whisk mixture until honey is dissolved.
Chill until ready to serve.
To serve, pour tea into ice-filled glasses.

name: Peppermint Patty Hot Chocolate
directions: Combine milk, syrup, and sugar in a sauce pan.
Warm over medium heat until hot.
In mugs, add 1-2 Tbsp.
of the Schnaps in each mug.
Pour the Hot Chocolate in the mugs leaving a 1/2 inch space.
Top each mug with Cool Whip.
Enjoy!

similar.name: Hot Cocoa Mix in a Mug

name: Cheap Liqueur
directions: Boil sugar and water for at least 5 minutes.
The longer you boil it, the thicker the liqueur will be-- but there will be a slight decrease in the amount.
Add coffee and vanilla.
If you can't find Camp's, use a very strong brand or an espresso type.
Allow mixture to cool before adding alcohol.
Pour into a glass container.

name: Diabetic Cafe au Lait
directions: Place coffee granules into coffee cup/mug.
In small saucepan, heat milk, sweet-n-low, and cinnamon over medium-low heat until it reaches a simmer.
Pour into coffee cup, stir well and enjoy!

similar.name: Low-Cal Iced Coffee With Milk (Cafe Au Lait)
Orange Cafe Au Lait Mix- Gift in a Jar

name: Caramel Macchiato
directions: You can use vanilla syrup from a bottle for the drink or make your own vanilla syrup following this Top Secret Recipe: Simply combine 2 cups water and 1 1/2 cups sugar in a medium saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes, then add 3/4 teaspoon vanilla extract.
Remove from heat and cool.
Store in a covered container.
To make your coffee drink, add two tablespoon of vanilla syrup to a 16-ounce glass.
Add 1/2 cup fresh brewed espresso followed by 8 ounces of steamed milk.
Add 3 to 4 tablespoons caramel sauce to the drink.
Stir before drinking.

name: Cocoa
directions: In a large heavy saucepan combine milk, cocoa powder and sugar.
Bring to a simmer over medium high heat, whisking frequently.
Add chocolate and continue whisking until melted and smooth.
Add salt.
Bring back to a simmer whisking constantly until frothy.
Ladle into warm mugs and top with marshmellows.

similar.name: Hot Cocoa Mix in a Mug
Quick and Easy, Chocolaty & Creamy, Hot Cocoa

name: Q.F Shooter
directions: Serve in a shooter glass.

name: Irish Cream Liqueur
directions: Dissolve coffee ganules in boiling water.
Using mixer, beat eggs in large mixing bowl for about 3 minutes.
Add coffee and remaining ingredients to the egg mixture.
Mix well.
Strain if desired.
Pour into air-tight glass container.
Store in refrigerator from 1 month or more.

similar.name: Bailey's Irish Cream Liqueur (Gift-Giving or for Yourself!)

name: Copycat Chili's Presidente Margarita
directions: Mix and serve over ice.
How much easier can it get.

name: Cold Brew Lime/Lemonade Tea
directions: Put tea bags into cold water, let sit 5 minutes.
Stir in lemonade or limeade concentrate.
Enjoy!

name: Egg Beater's Eggnog
directions: Whip Egg Beaters and Equal together and combine with two types of milk and flavorings.
Mix well.
Chill overnight if possible.
Pour into tumblers and dust with nutmeg.

name: Bloody Mary Mix
directions: In a pitcher, combine all ingredients.
Chill.
Serve over ice.
Garnish with celery stalks and lemon wedges.

name: Mexican Coffee
directions: Remove rind from one orange.
Cut the rind into slivers and the remaining orange into 8 slices (reserved for garnish).
Combine slivered rind, cloves, cinnamon pieces, and coffee in the basket of a coffee maker.
Add 8 cups of water and brew.
Meanwhile, heat milk to simmering in a small saucepan.
Pour into a blender and whirl milk 1 minute until frothy.
Serve milk from a pitcher with the hot coffee.
Garnish mugs with orange slices.

name: Cinnamon Cocoa Coffee
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Store in airtight container no longer than 6 months.
For one serving: 1 cup hot water and 2 to 3 tablespoons coffee mix.

similar.name: Crock-Pot Hot Cocoa for a Group
Cinnamon Mocha Coffee from Taste of Home

name: Vanilla Coco Iced Coffee
directions: Boil water.
Add coffee.
Boil for a few more minutes.
Remove from heat.
Put all the ingredients in a blender, except the ice cream, and blend well.
Refrigerate.
Serve chilled in 4 glasses with a scoop of ice cream on top and a sprinkle of a little cocoa powder!
Enjoy this pretty coffee with a sweet smile on your lips!
Drink away!

similar.name: Charishma's Divine Mocha Nescafe Frothe Coffee

name: Dairy-Free Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Combine powdered soymilk, sugar, cocoa and salt in large, airtight container.
For each serving, add 1/4 cup cocoa mixture into mug with 8 oz. boiling water; stir until well mixed.
Garnish with Cool Whip or other dairy-free whipped cream substitute, if desired.

name: Nescafe Star (Ideal Mix)
directions: Put the first 3 ingredients in a mug.
Stir in hot water.
Serve hot.

name: Nescafe Viennese Finesse
directions: Combine the first 3 ingredients.
Pour into 2 heat-proof glasses.
Top with a dollop of whipped cream and serve hot.

name: Nescafe African Passion
directions: Blend everything in a mixer for 1 minute and serve hot.

name: Nescafe Choco Mint
directions: Combine everything in a blender.
Process until smooth.
Pour into a mug, garnish with chocolate shavings and serve hot!

name: Beautiful Nescafe Oriental Spring
directions: Combine everything in a blender.
Pour into a mug.
Sprinkle with grated chocolate and serve hot.

name: Iced Nescafe Deluxe
directions: Combine everything in a blender/mixie.
Process until smooth.
Pour into a glass over 1-2 ice cubes, sprinkle with grated chocolate and serve cold.

name: Nescafe Morning Essential
directions: Put into a blender.
Process until smooth.
Pour into a mug and serve hot.

name: Dark Chocolate Coffee
directions: Pour half a cup of milk into a microwave proof container.
Place it in the microwave on HIGH power level for 30 seconds.
Remove from microwave.
Now, melt the chocolate in the microwave on HIGH power level for 30 seconds.
Remove and keep aside.
Prepare a mixture of coffee powder, melted chocolate, sugar and a little milk.
Whisk well to ensure that there are no lumps.
It should be frothy as well.
Add a drop of hazelnut syrup.
Grab a serving glass.
Put 1 tbsp.
of the coffee mixture in it and pour half a cup of hot milk over it.
Mix well.
Prepare foam in the hot cup of cold milk with a foamer and pour into the glass.
Sprinkle cinnamon powder over it and serve rightaway.
Similarly, prepare more glasses of delicious chocolate coffee with the remaining milk and coffee mixture.

similar.name: Jenn's Sugar Free Dark Chocolate Orange Faux Chai Latte Coffee

name: Garden Bloody Mary
directions: Cut an X in the bottom of the tomatoes and stew for 3 to 5 minutes in boiling water or until the skins start to peel away.
Drain and place in ice water.
Peel the skins off tomatoes and cut any hard or brown areas away.
Put the tomatoes in the blender along with cilantro, garlic and salt.
Blend.
Pour a shot of vodka into a glass filled with ice.
The next part is all depending on taste and the ingredients are per glass.
This is how I like it.
Add Worcestershire sauce, pickle juice, olive juice, celery salt, salt, pepper, and Tabasco.
Drop in a pickle spear and stir with a celery stick.

name: The Coffee Cooler
directions: Fill a cocktail shaker with ice.
Add all ingredients, shake well& pour into a glass.
Sprinkle with shaved chocolate.

name: Perfect Iced Tea
directions: In a 4-cup measuring cup, heat 4 cups water in microwave to boiling.
Place tea bags in water and let steep for 5- 10 minutes.
Pour sugar into 2 quart pitcher and pour hot tea over it.

Squeeze tea bags to get out any remaining tea; add tea to pitcher and discard bags (if they are too hot to squeeze by hand, pull them up the side of the measuring cup with the strings and press with a spoon).
Stir hot tea until sugar is dissolved.
Add 4 cups cold water and lemon juice; stir well.
Chill until very cold and serve over ice with lemon.

similar.name: Easy Iced Tea Otherwise Referred to As Fas-T
Southern Sweet Iced Tea, Hotel Style

name: Hot Buttered Rum Mix
directions: Soften butter and mix with brown sugar, cinnamon, nutmeg and vanilla.
Refrigerate or freeze.
For each serving, use 1 tablespoon of the mix, 1/2 ounce to 1 1/2 ounces of rum and 6 ounces boiling water.

name: Bailey's Irish Cream Liqueur (Gift-Giving or for Yourself!)
directions: In blender, combine all ingredients; blend until smooth.
Store tightly covered in refrigerator up to one month. (It won't last that long!) Shake bottle well before serving, and return remaining liqueur to refrigerator.
Note: Use only Grade A, uncracked eggs.

name: The Nutty Chocolate Coffee
directions: Place chocolate squares into 2 heatproof mugs.
Cover each with 2 oz hot coffee& stir until chocolate has partially dissolved.
Add almond extract, balance of the hot coffee& the hot milk; stir again.
Garnish with whipped cream& shaved chocolate.

name: The Caesar Celebration (A Mocktail)
directions: Use 1 lime wedge to wet rim of tall glass.
Combine celery salt and pepper in a shallow dish and turn glass upside down in mixture to coat rim.
Fill the glass with ice, add hot sauce, Worcestershire, tomato-clam juice& juice from other lime wedge and stir well.
Garnish with celery stick.

name: Healthy(Er) Eggnog
directions: Refrigerate liquids in advance.
Beat the egg substitute for 2 or 3 minutes with an electric mixer at medium speed until very frothy.
Gradually beat in the sugar, vanilla and nutmeg.
Stir in the milk by hand.
Chill before serving.
Let guests add their own rum or brandy or a sprinkle of nutmeg, if desired.

name: HOLIDAY ORANGE EGGNOG wow!
directions: In large punch bowl, stir together the eggnog, milk, orange juice concentrate and sugar.
Float scoops of ice cream in the eggnog mixture.
Sprinkle with nutmeg.

name: Lovely and Luscious Mug of Coffee
directions: Place both coffees in bottom of mug.
Add hot water.
Stir.
Add sugar to taste (I like my coffee without sugar).
Stir.
Add cream.
Stir.
Aaaaaaahhhhhh!
Note below: You can get a Recipezaar mug at CafePress.com (just go to http://www. cafeshops. com/recipezaar. 6946752).

name: Frothy Spiced Cocoa
directions: Whisk 1 cup of the milk with the remaining ingredients in a bowl.
Heat the rest of the milk in a heavy pot.
When hot, but not boiling, whisk in the cocoa mixture.
Bring to a boil, lower heat& simmer gently for 3 minutes.
Let cool for 1 minute.
Whisk (or froth with a cappucino frother) just before serving.
Garnish with a cinnamon stick.

similar.name: Instant Hot Cocoa With a Kick!

name: Mocha Vanilla Cocoa Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together and blend, a few cups at a time, in a blender or food processer to make a fine powder.
To use, add 1 cup of hot water to ¼ cup of Cocoa Mix.

name: Hot Chocolate Mix Fix
directions: Add Half& Half to mixture and stir.
This will cure the nasty, watery texture and add to the creaminess that hot chocolate used to have in"the Good Ol' Days".

similar.name: Creamy Hot Chocolate Mix in a Jar (For Gift-Giving)

name: Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: In a large bowl, combine the nonfat dry milk and the powdered sugar.
Stir thoroughly to get rid of lumps in powdered sugar.
Add the other ingredients and mix well.
Make a cup (3 tbsp. mix to 1 cup hot water) to sample.
For more chocolate taste, add more cocoa.
To make it sweeter, add more powdered sugar.
Store in airtight container in a cool, dry place.
Makes about 9 cups of mix.
Use within about 4 months.

name: Maple Coffee
directions: Combine half and half and syrup in a sauce pan over medium heat, stirring constantly until thoroughly heated (do not boil!).
Stir in coffee.
Serve with sweet whipped cream.

name: Sneak-Up Margarita
directions: Pour the 1 tablespoon of lime juice onto a saucer. Spread it around the saucer.
Pour the tablespoon of salt onto another saucer.
Dip a glass into the lime juice and then in the salt. You can skip this step if you don't want salt on the rim.
Place ice cubes into the glass
Pour the rest of the ingredients into the glass and stir well.
You can also put the ingredients in a blender and blend well for a frozen/blended margarita.
Garnish with a lime wedge for a nice little touch!
Cheers!

name: Lime-Mint Tea
directions: Bring 4 cups of water to a boil in large saucepan.  Pour over tea bags.  Cover and steep 5 minutes; discard tea bags.  Stir in mint let stand 15 minutes.

Pour tea through a wire mash strainer into a bowl; discard mint.  Bring remaining 4 1/2 cups of water and sugar to a boil in a saucepan; cool.  Stir in tea and juices.  Chill and serve over ice.  garnish if desired.

name: Cherry Mocha Coffee, low-carb
directions: I make large servings which are close to 16 oz. coffee each, so make this in a LARGE mug.
Multiply the recipe for the number of people you are serving.

Make the whipped cream while waiting for coffee to brew. In a bowl add all other ingredients (cream, flavorings, sweetener and cocoa). Use an electric mixer to whip the cream until soft peaks form. Do not overwhip as it will quickly turn to butter consistency.
Put whipping cream mixture in mug. Pour hot coffee over cream and stir. For best results, mix thoroughly.

If you are making for several people and some people want sugar and some want Splenda, make the whipped cream without the sweetener. Instead, add the 2 Tblsp. sweetener to each cup, as preferred. Pour a little coffee over the sweetener and mix. Then add the cream to each cup. Then fill the mug the rest of the way with hot coffee and stir.

similar.name: Coffee Slushie, Low Sugar  -  Fat Free

name: Charishma's Vanilla Chai
directions: Pour milk and water in a microwave-safe and microwave-proof cup.
Heat in the microwave for 1 minute.
Add sugar and heat again for 1 minute.
Stir in the Chai Latta mix.
Heat for 1 minute.
Remove from microwave and strain into another clean cup.
Add a straw into the cup and sip sip sip!
ENJOY!

similar.name: Better Chai from a Chai Tea Bag

name: Mocha Freeze
directions: Warm 2 cups of milk and stir in sugar, coffee and cocoa until desolved.
Combine remaining ingredients.
Freeze until slushy (approx. 5 hours).

name: Val's Eggnog
directions: Beat whipping cream with electric mixer until it forms soft peaks, then add Eagle Brand Condensed Milk and whole milk.
Add in beaten eggs.
Sprinkle with nutmeg.

name: Charishma's Divine Mocha Nescafe Frothe Coffee
directions: Pour water and milk in a microwave-safe and microwave-proof cup.
Heat for 1 minute in the microwave.
Then, stir in the sugar.
Heat, once again, for 1 minute in the microwave.
Add the coffee powder, mix well, and heat for 1 minute in the microwave.
Remove, stir well, transfer into another cup and make sure all the coffee granules have dissolved.
If not, mix well and heat for another minute.
Serve alongside Creme de Pirouline Chocolate Hazelnut cookies{my favourite combo with this coffee!
}Enjoy the aroma and taste of your creamy, foamy, simply divine coffee!

name: Instant Coffee Espresso
directions: Put the coffee powder and sugar in a coffee mug.

Add a few drops of water (just enough to incorporate the coffee powder in to form a thick paste) Stir vigorously with a spoon and soon you will see that the coffe and sugar form a whitish shiny paste.
Boil the milk and water together.

(if you do not like milky coffee you can boil plain water (1 cup) and add a few drops of milk just to your taste straight into the mug) Pour this from a little height into the mug so that the coffee froths up.
Sprinkle a little drinking chocolate on top for effect.
You can sprinkle some cinnamon powder on top too if you like the flavour of cinnamon.

name: Iced Sweet Mint Tea
directions: In large tea pot (or two small tea pots) pour boiling water over tea bags and mint and allow to steep for 20 minutes.
Place sugar in 2-quart pitcher (that will tolerate some heat).
Pour freshly brewed tea (minus mint and tea bags) into sugar and stir until sugar is dissolved.
Place in refrigerator until ready to serve.
To serve, pour into tall glasses over ice.
Serve with lemon slices.
Serves 8.

name: Coffee Syrup for One
directions: Stir together coffee and brown sugar in small pan; then gently boil, uncovered, for about 10 minutes until slightly thickened.
Stir in vanilla and butter.
Serve warm.
Variations: Add 1-2 teaspoons bourbon or coffee liqueur (Kahlua is wonderful) or stir in 1-2 teaspoons sour cream.

similar.name: Hot Buttered Rum Coffee - Non Alcoholic

name: Spiced Strawberry Lemonade Tea
directions: Bring tea bags and water to a boil; let steep at least 30 minutes.
Pour still hot tea into a half gallon pitcher; add the drink mix.
Stir until drink mix is dissolved.
Fill pitcher up the rest of the way with cold water; enjoy.

I would say if you like it sweeter, you would be better off adding a bit of sugar to the mixture as opposed to more lemonade or it will begin to taste simply like the lemonade and you would lose the spiced tea flavor.

similar.name: Southern Sweet Iced Tea, Hotel Style

name: French Vanilla Cocoa Mix in a Jar
directions: Combine all ingredients and ladle into jars.
Cover tightly.
Attach the following directions on a gift tag: French Vanilla Cocoa: Stir 3 heaping tablespoons of cocoa mix into hot water or milk.

name: Hot Mexican Spiced Cocoa
directions: Mix first 4 items together, in a good sized mug.
Add boiling water, stir till smooth.
Heat milk for 3 minutes over LOW heat until hot.
Stir the milk into the cocoa mixture until well mixed.
Stir in vanilla and almond extracts.
Enjoy--.

name: Southern Limeade
directions: Mix first two ingredients together.
serve over ice.
note: I use the frozen limeade concentrate, it is very good even in the generic brands.

similar.name: The Recipe (From the Baldwin Sisters on Walton's Mountain)

name: Refreshingly Cool Almond Tea
directions: Dissolve tea and sugar in warm water.
Add remaining ingredients and stir until mixed.
Chill until served.

name: Homemade Hot Cocoa (Penzeys Recipe)
directions: Combine milk, sugar, cocoa, and vanilla in saucepan.
Whisk or stir quickly to dissolve cocoa into milk.
Warm over med-low heat till steaming, stirring frequently.
Pour into mugs, top with mini-marshmallows, and use cinnamon sticks to stir.

similar.name: Quick and Easy, Chocolaty & Creamy, Hot Cocoa

name: Light Coffee Frappuccino
directions: Put all ingredients into a blender.
Use the ice crush mode for a slushy texture.
Blend to your own preference.

name: Northside Special
directions: Squeeze orange into 12-ounce collins glass.
Add sugar, lemon juice, rum and cracked ice.
Fill glass with soda water.
Stir slightly and serve with straws.

name: Russian Tea Mix (Orange Spiced Tea)
directions: Mix all ingredients together and store in a covered container.
To use, dissolve 2 tsp mix in one cup of hot water.

name: Champagne Cocktail
directions: Put 2 drops of bitters into a champagne glass, then Grand Marnier.
Fill rest of glass with Champagne.

name: Mama's Eggnog
directions: In a bowl beat the egg yolks with the 1/2 cup of sugar until thick.
In another bowl beat the egg whites with 1/4 cup of sugar until thick.
In a third bowl beat the cream until thick.
Add the cream to the yolks, fold in the egg whites, and add the milk, Bourbon, vanilla, and a pinch of nutmeg, if desired.
Chill in freezer before serving.
Serve eggnog in a large punch bowl.

name: Baileys Butterball
directions: Place topping and chocolate on separate plates.
Dip rims of 4 serving glasses in topping, then in chocolate.
Place on a tray lined with non-stick baking paper, rim-side down and refrigerate for 5 minutes or until set.
Combine Baileys and cream in separate jug.
Divide butterscotch schnapps evenly among serving glasses.
Top with ice.
Pour over combined Baileys and cream.
Serve immediately.

similar.name: Irish Cream Recipe (Taste Matched to Baileys)

name: Russian Tea (non-tea)
directions: In a large saucepan, bring 2 cups of water to a boil.
Remove from heat, then stir in ground cinnamon, ground ginger, allspice, and ground cloves.
Allow mixture to steep for 10 minutes.
Prepare frozen orange juice concentrate according to the directions on the can.
Dissolve 1 cup of sugar in 1 cup of water, and stir into spice mixture.
Mix in orange juice, lemon juice, and 5 cups water.
Heat through.
Serve with a garnish of orange zest.

name: Tomato Juice Warmer
directions: Pour tomato juice into a microwaveable mug.
Microwave on high for 1-2 minutes (depending on power of the microwave).
Remove from microwave and add rest of ingredients.
Stir and add lemon slice as garnish.

name: Mama's Old-Fashioned Russian Tea
directions: Boil water with cloves and cinnamon sticks for 20 minutes in large non-aluminum pot.
Remove from heat, add the tea bags and cover.
Allow mixture to"steep" for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight.
After steeping process is completed, combine in another sauce pan the orange, pineapple, and lemon juices with the sugar.
Heat gently, stirring to melt and mix the sugar.
Drain spices from tea mixture.
Combine the tea and juice mixtures.
Heat and enjoy!
NOTE: Preparation time does not include"steeping time".

name: Spiced Tea
directions: Boil water.
add tea bags, cloves, one cinnamon stick and reduce heat.
Simmer for 3-5 minutes.
Remove tea bags, cloves, and cinnamon stick.
Stir in cranberry juice, lemon and honey.
Heat completely.
Serve in four mugs and garnish with cinnamon sticks.

name: Easy Cocoa Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together well.
Store in airtight container.
Decorate the jar.
Instructions for the jar.
Use 3 to 4 heaping tablespoons of mix per cup of boiling water.

similar.name: French Vanilla Cocoa Mix in a Jar

name: Vegan Hot Chocolate
directions: Combine cocoa and sugar and mix to get rid of any lumps.
Put in a pan with 1/2 cup of the boiling water.
Whisk, and simmer gently for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Dissolve the creamed coconut in remaining water.
Add coconut/water to pan and simmer for another minute.
Add cinnamon and vanilla if using.

similar.name: Vegan Hot Cocoa - No Dairy or Soy
Low Carb, Low Sugar Hot Cocoa
The Best Hot Chocolate Ever - No Sugar

name: Vegan Hot Cocoa - No Dairy or Soy
directions: Add All of the ingredients in a microwaveable mug and microwave on high for 1 minute.
Stir and drink.

name: Creamy Cocoa Mix (Extra-Large Batch)
directions: Mix all ingredients in a large bowl and blend with a whisk.
Store in airtight container.
Combine, to taste, with hot water or milk.

similar.name: Creamy Hot Chocolate Mix in a Jar (For Gift-Giving)
French Vanilla Cocoa Mix in a Jar

name: Southern Sweet Iced Tea, Hotel Style
directions: Gather supplies for tea.
Realize that you are missing a measuring cup, and your pitcher is broken.
Find replacement tools (coffee pot works great for measuring cup, and glass beer jugs for pitchers, and an automotive funnel to compensate for lack of a pitcher with a wide opening).
Carefully measure 2 cups water using coffee pot, bring to a boil.
Prepare 2 cups of sugar, funnel into large beer jug.
Pour boiling water into coffee pot, realize you are now down to 1 cup of water-oh yea, vapor-- ooops.
Fill pot back up, bring to a boil again.
Carefully pour boiling water into coffee pot till you hit the 2 cup mark.
Add in Two"family size tea bags" and a pinch of baking soda, place lid on coffee pot, place beer jug on top to cover most of the holes.
After 15 minutes, remove tea bags, do not squeeze into pot, discard.
Use funnel to add lukewarm teawater to sugar mix, ponder how bad of an idea it is to try to dissolve 2 cups of sugar into 2 cups of warm water.
Cap beer jug, shake furiously, realize it is only halfway dissolved, fret.
Pour mixture back into the coffee pot, stir vigorously.
Decide the lukewarm water is what is killing you, place pot onto the coffee machine, turn on to heat water.
Remove occasionally to check water warmth, stir often.
Pour concentrated mixture into funnel aimed into another beer jug, with 6 cups of cold water.
Cap, and shake well.
Serve over ice.
Cough due to sever sugar content, resolve to make the next batch better.
Add water to put, bring to a boil.
Measure 1 1/2 cups of sugar, and pour into ceramic beer stein.
When water is boiling use coffee pot to measure two cups.
Gently pour into beer stein, stir vigorously till sugar is dissolved, do a small victory dance.
Add a pinch of baking soda, and two family sized tea bags.
Use a lid from a small pot, place upside down onto the stein to allow mixture to steep.
After allowing to steep for 15 minutes, use funnel to pour into beer jug#2, with 6 cups of cold water.
Cap, shake well, serve over ice.
Loudly proclaim victory over the tea, and decide that next round, you might just go for 1 1/4 cups of sugar.

name: Fireside Holiday Coffee
directions: Mix all ingredients until well blended.
Store in air tight container.
For each individual cup use 2 tablespoons mix to 1 cup boiling water.
For a pretty presentation, top with fresh whipped cream.
If making this for a large crowd, this whole recipe will make a full 30 cup coffeemaker of holiday coffee that adults and kids alike will enjoy!

similar.name: Cinnamon Mocha Coffee from Taste of Home

name: Strawberry Lemonade
directions: In blender add lemon juice, frozen strawberries, 1 cup water and honey.
Blend until smooth.
Add to pitcher along with remainder of water.
Stir until thoroughly mixed and refrigerate.
Serve chilled.

name: Mom's Russian Tea
directions: Mix all ingredients together thoroughly.  Use approximately 3 tsp for a serving.
You may substitute an instant tea that is already sweetened (Lipton's brand is yummy) for the tea and sugar above.

name: Rum Martini
directions: Mix ingredients in a cocktail shaker with ice.
Shake well.
Strain into a chilled martini glass.
Serve with a stuffed olive or cocktail onion on martini pick.
Twist a lemon peel over the drink but do not drop.

name: Homemade Irish Baileys
directions: Mix coffee with the water.
Mix coffee mixture, whiskey, cream, condensed milk and eggs together.
Keeps in the refrigerator for about a week.

similar.name: Irish Cream Recipe (Taste Matched to Baileys)

name: Mocha Nightcap
directions: Heat milk, chocolate and coffee to boiling in a medium jug, about 2-5mins.
Stir well, add the brandy, pour into a glass or mug, top with whipped cream and sprinkle the cinnamon.

name: Irish Coffee
directions: Pour the Irish Whiskey into a warm glass or mug.
Fill the mug with the coffee.
Stir in the cream and sugar to get the taste you desire.

name: Chai (East African Tea)
directions: Heat together water and tea leaves in a 3 to 4 quart saucepan.
Boil 10 minutes. Add milk and heat to near boiling. Strain leaves. Add sugar to taste.

similar.name: Better Chai from a Chai Tea Bag

name: Simply Delicious--best Margarita Ever
directions: Mix first ingredients and serve over ice. YUMMMMMMMMMM.

name: Simple Hot Mocha
directions: Brew coffee using 1 cup water and 1/4 cup coffee grounds.
pour 1/4 cup of brewed coffee in a mug.
add 1/2 cup ice cream or milk (ice cream makes it sweeter, for you sweet-tooths like me).
add the chocolate syrup, and stir until everything is blended. it may be necessary to put the mixture in a blender, or heat in the microwave to melt the ice cream.
enjoy!

name: Hot Spiced Afternoon Tea for 25--30
directions: Add spices together with water to a large sacuepan and bring to a full rolling boil.  Remove from heat.
IMMEDIATELY add tea bags and brew for 4--5 minutes.  Stir and discard tea bags.
Add sugar, stir until dissolved.
Add fruit juices.
To reheat for serving, place over low heat. DO NOT BOIL!  Place into a punch bowl or tea urn and serve in china or ceramic cups.

name: Amaretto Iced Coffee
directions: Combine everything except ice in a blender and whip until smooth.
Fill glasses half full with crushed ice or a few ice cubes, and fill the rest with coffee mixture.
Sprinkle with some cinnamon or cacao.
Enjoy!

similar.name: Low-Cal Iced Coffee With Milk (Cafe Au Lait)

name: Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: Put dry milk into a large mixing bowl.
Combine the sugar, cocoa and salt, and sift or sieve the mixture into the dry milk- Blend thoroughly.
To make hot chocolate: Put 5 tablespoons of the mix into a mug or large cup. Slowly stir in hot water, stirring until the mix is dissolved and the cup is full.

name: Mom's Best Sweet Iced Tea
directions: Fill the coffee maker with the maximum amount of water.
Put the tea bag in the place where you would normally put the coffee and filter.
After it has finished brewing pour the tea into a gallon sized jug.
Add sugar and stir.
Add cold, fresh water to one gallon mark.
Refrigerate and enjoy!

similar.name: Southern Sweet Iced Tea, Hotel Style

name: Sugar-Free Flavored Tea
directions: Put water in large pot and bring to a boil.
Put tea bags in boiling water and simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove pot from heat and stir in the Splenda and extract.
Pour tea in pitcher or other container and serve immediately over ice or refrigerate.
Note: If you're using an electric tea maker follow manufacturer's instructions and skip to step 3.

name: Swamp Water
directions: Fill a pint canning jar with ice halfway up.  Add limeade, brandy, vodka top with 7 up.  instead of the 7 up and limeade sour may be used.

name: Mexican Coffee Mix
directions: Mix together.
Store in a jar or covered container.
To use, stir 3 tablespoons into a mug of boiling water.

name: Sugar Free Cocoa
directions: Stir together.
Store in airtight jar.
Use 1/3 cup mix in a mug of boiling water and stir.
Add 1 teaspoons instant coffee to mug for mocha.

name: Sassafras Tea
directions: Gather or buy the sassafrass root. Wash roots and cut saplings off where they're green and where the root ends.
Bring water to a boil and add roots.
Simmer until the water is a deep brownish red (the darker the stronger -- I like mine strong).
Strain into a pitcher through wire and a coffee filter if you don't want any sediment.
Add honey or sugar to taste.
Serve hot or cold (I like mine ice cold) with lemon and a sprig of mint.

name: Mango Iced Tea
directions: Heat the water and brew the tea as directed.
Add the sugar and stir to dissolve.  Cool slightly.
Pour in the mango nectar and give a good stir.
Serve over ice or chill before serving.  I usually leave off the lemon in this cup o' tea.

name: Ginger-Cider Lemonade
directions: Bring the water, sugar, and fresh ginger to a boil in a medium saucepan.  Boil for 1 minute, then remove from heat.
Add lemons to the pan and let stand for 30 minutes.  Squeeze juice from the lemons.
Strain the lemon mixture through a fine sieve into a large bowl or pitcher.  Discard the solids.
Stir in the cider and chill for at least 1 hour before serving.

name: Mucho Gusto Gump Margarita
directions: Blend all in a blender.
Wipe the glass rim with a wedge of lime and then dredge the rim in a mixture of 1/2 salt and 1/2 sugar mixed together. If you are reading this recipe --  you know how to season a margarita glass.
Note: the egg white is integral in this drink. It makes it nice and frothy like an Orange Julius. Please don't omit it.

name: Cinnamon-Vanilla Coffee Creamer
directions: Place all ingredients except vanilla in food processor.
Blend for about 30 seconds while slowly dripping in vanilla extract.
May add/subtract flavoring to suit your tastes, I've added almond extract to this as well and it is wonderful.
*Note* if mixing in bowl, SLOWLY add the extract as it clumps the mixture easily.

name: Mexican Hot Cocoa
directions: In a small heavy-duty saucepan; combine sugar, cocoa and cinnamon.
Over medium heat, gradually stir in milk and stir constantly until hot, do not boil.
Remove from heat and stir in vanilla extract. Beat with a wire whisk until frothy and pour into cups. Serve immediately.

similar.name: Instant Hot Cocoa With a Kick!

name: Perfect Everyday Iced Coffee
directions: Brew your coffee double strength by using 2 tablespoons of ground coffee with 3/4 cup water.
Fill a tall glass with ice.  A normal sized travel mug works well, but don't use anything smaller than that.
After the coffee has brewed, pour it into a ceramic mug or other heat-safe container.
Stir in sugar.  This avoids the sugar at the bottom of the cup which usually happens at coffee shops.
Pour over ice.  You'd think it would be best to chill the coffee first, but it cools down immediately and melts down enough of the ice that it becomes normal strength.
Add half and half, or your usual cream.
Try this recipe a few more times; fiddle around with it.  I think this should make a good base recipe for most people.

name: Sugar Free Hot Cocoa
directions: Stir together dry milk powder, cocoa, and Splenda in a mug. Pour in the boiling water and stir until dissolved.

name: Cinnamon & Ouzo Iced Tea
directions: Place the cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, sugar and tea bags in a large heatproof jug, add the boiling water and stir until the sugar dissolves; set aside for 3 minutes to infuse; remove the teabag and discard.
Stir in the ouzo and place the tea in the fridge for 2 hours to chill.
To serve: Place the ice cubes in serving glasses and pour over the chilled tea.

name: Homemade Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Store in self locking bags or airtight containers.
TO serve: Add 1/4- 1/2 Cup of mix to 1 mug of very hot water.

name: Frozen Coffee Granita
directions: In a bowl stir together hot coffee and sugar and stir until the sugar is dissolved, then add in vanilla.
Pour into an 8-inch metal baking pan and freeze, stirring every 30 minutes, until slushy (this should take about 1-1/2 hours).
Transfer mixture to a bowl and stir vigorously with a fork until slightly smoother and more uniform in texture (about 30 seconds).
Return to the freezer until firm enough to scoop (about another 30-40 minutes.
Divide the granita into serving dishes and top with whipped cream.

similar.name: Iced Coffee With Ice Coffee Cubes

name: Spiced Cream Coffee or Spiced Cream Irish Coffee
directions: Whip the cream with the nutmeg, sugar, and 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon.
Divide the coffee and whiskey (if desired) into 2 cups.
Stir 1 teaspoon chocolate syrup and 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon into each cup; top with spiced cream.

name: Frozen Irish Coffee
directions: Mix all ingredients, except whipped cream, in a blender until smooth.
Pour into a mug and top with whipped cream.

similar.name: If You Can Boil Water Irish Coffee

name: My Best "original" Bloody Mary
directions: Mix all of the ingredients together in a large pitcher except the celery and lime wedges. Add plenty of ice.

If you use the celery, process it very finely. Then take a clean piece of cloth or a dish towel and place celery in the middle. Wrap the cloth around the celery and squeeze, while twisting for the juice. Add the juice to the mix. I know this may seem time consuming though in my opinion the flavor is more fresh and appealing than using celery salt.
Garnish with lime wedges or any of the myriad garnishes you like for bloody mary's, sit back, relax and enjoy!

name: The Best Hot Chocolate Ever - No Sugar
directions: Heat up water and milk in a cocoa mug to very hot in microwave or on stove.
Add both cocoa and splenda.
Stir well and serve.

similar.name: Low Carb, Low Sugar Hot Cocoa

name: Blue Martini Ice Pops
directions: In a saucepan simmer water, sugar and lemon zest until sugar is dissolved.
Cool the syrup, then stir in gin, vermouth and Curacao; discard the lemon zest.
Pour into molds and freeze until the mixture is slushy.
Insert the sticks, then return to freezer.

name: Spiced Iced Tea
directions: Bring the sugar and water to a boil.
Tie spices in a small bag and drop spice bag in the water and let it boil for one minute.
Remove from heat and add the tea.  Let stand ten minutes.
Strain the tea mixture into a large container.
Add the frozen juices (they do not need to be completely thawed).
Stir to blend.  Store in refrigerator.  Serve cold.
Time to make does not include time to cool.

similar.name: Southern Sweet Iced Tea, Hotel Style

name: Maple Hot Chocolate (Or Mint)
directions: In a large saucepan, combine sugar, cocoa and salt.  Stir in hot water and butter;  bring to a boil.
Add the milk, maple (or mint) flavoring, vanilla and 8 marshmallows.  Heat through, stirring occasionally, until marshmallows are melted.
Ladle into mugs and top each with a marshmallow.

similar.name: Heaven in a Cup Hot Chocolate

name: Bloody Mary Slushie
directions: Cut up the tomato (remove the tough green stalk parts) and the celery.
Throw everything into a blender and add cold water to make one pint (0.45 litre).
Mix for a while.
Adjust the seasoning and serve.
The slush can be strained, but the coarse texture is part of the idea.

name: Thick and Chocolatey Hot Chocolate
directions: combine cocoa, cornflour and sugar, and mix until combined.
Add a small bit of milk to for a runny paste, add rest of milk.
Stir over hear until it boils.
divide between 4 mugs and top up with cold milk.
Stir in some liquor of your choice for an adult version!

name: Fast Low-Carb Hot Chocolate (Aztec Style)
directions: Melt the chocolate in the microwave about 2 minutes at 50% power.
Stir in vanilla extract, Splenda, heavy whipping cream, cayenne, chili, and cinnamon.
Add hot water and serve.
Makes 1 delicious serving.

name: Sugar Free Eggnog
directions: Mix milk, egg substitute and pudding together, adding a dash of spices.
For extra decadance, I add French Vanilla whip cream!
When drink is frothy, either pour into a cup, add another dash of nutmeg and enjoy,.
or refrigerate until cold.
I like to use an immersion blender to mix ("boat motor").
I also like to add hot chocolate mix to my egg nog, so I'll bet that chocolate pudding would be yummy too.
Be careful if heating for a hot drink - the eggs get a little curdled.
"Cook time" is chill time.

similar.name: Light and Creamy Low Fat and Sugar Free Eggnog

name: Cosmopolitan
directions: Keep all ingredients in the fridge for a few hours.
Shake with 5-6 ice cubes and sieve into a cocktail glass.
Enjoy.

name: Viennese Coffee
directions: Combine the hot coffee, chocolate syrup and sugar in slow cooker.
Cover and cook on low 2 to 2 1/2 hours.
Stir in heavy cream and creme de cacao.
Cover and cook 30 minutes or until heated through.
Ladle into tall coffee mugs, top with whipped cream and garnish with chocolate shavings.

name: Bavarian Mint Coffee
directions: In small bowl combine nondairy creamer, sugar, instant coffee, and cocoa. Set aside.
Finely crush candies in food processor, blender, or with tenderizing mallet. Add to dry mixture.
Store in airtight container and if giving as a gift, include a label that says to use 2 to 2½ teaspoons per cup of boiling water.

name: Russian Tea--Rachel's Version
directions: Mix all ingredients together and store in a tightly covered jar.
To serve, mix about 3 teaspoons of mix with 1 cup boiling water.

name: Russian Tea
directions: Combine all ingredients.  Store in tightly covered jar.
To serve, mix about 3 teaspoons mix with 1 cup boiling water.

name: Ginger Cinnamon Tea
directions: In a saucepan, simmer ginger, honey, cinnamon, and water for 20 minutes.  Simmer longer for stronger tea.
Add honey or sugar and srain tea through a sieve.

name: Sugar-Free Hot Cocoa Mix (With Splenda)
directions: Combine all ingredients (sift for a finer mix).
Store in an airtight container.
Add 1/4 cup mix and boiling water to a mug.
Stir to dissolve.

name: Easy Instant Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: Combine all ingredients, mixing well.  Store in an airtight container.
To make hot chocolate:  For each serving, place 1/3 cup of the mix in a mug or thick glass.  Add 1 cup boiling water and stir well.

name: Cool Mint Hot Chocolate
directions: Prepare hot chocolate according to package insturctions.
Drop in one peppermint patty and stir as it desolves.
Enjoy by the fire or outside on a cold day.

similar.name: Heaven in a Cup Hot Chocolate

name: Hot Buttered Rum Batter
directions: For batter:.
In a large mixing bowl, cream the butter and the sugars together until smooth.
Add the softened ice cream and spices and mix until smooth.
Transfer this to a freezer container with a tight fitting lid.
Place in the freezer for up to 1 month.
To serve:.
Place 2-3 heaping tablespoons of the frozen mix in a coffee mug.
Add 1 shot of dark rum and 6 ounces of boiling water and stir until the mixture is melted.
Sprinkle top with cinnamon or nutmeg.

name: Ooey Gooey Caramel Cocoa
directions: Dissolve cocoa into 6-8oz. water.
Add caramel, stir, and top with whipped cream.

similar.name: Double Chocolate Cocoa Mix in a Quart Jar

name: Low Carb, Low Sugar Hot Cocoa
directions: Mix cocoa & sugar together in saucepan.
Add water. Stir well.
Cook over med. heat until mixture comes to a boil and the sugar is dissolved.
Stir in milk, extracts and cinnamon.
Reduce heat to low & stir until warmed through.
Sit back, wrap your hands around the warm cup and enjoy.

name: Kahlua Hot Chocolate
directions: Heat the chocolate, milk and half and half cream over medium heat in a heavy saucepan, stirring until the chocolate has melted.
Whisk until the mixture is hot and frothy.
Add in sugar if desired.
Add in the Kahlua and vanilla; contine to whisk until the mixture is hot.
Remove from heat.
Pour into mugs and top with whipped cream.
Garnish with shaved chocolate.
Delicious!

name: Hot Cocoa
directions: Hot---Put milk in an stove-safe container.
Warm the milk and add the chocolate syrup and stir.
Serve.
Cold---Put milkin a cup and stir with chocolate syrup.

name: Eggnog
directions: In a large mixing bowl, beat the eggs with an electric mixer.
Add all other ingredients except the cinnamon and nutmeg and mix them in with the mixer.
Sprinkle with cinnamon and nutmeg and serve in tall glasses.

name: If You Can Boil Water Irish Coffee
directions: Make a cup of instant coffee using twice the recommend amount of instant coffee.
Add sugar and instant cocoa; stir until dissolved.
Add a shot of good Irish whiskey.
Top with Cool Whip or whipped cream.
Sprinkle with cinnamon.

name: Perfectly Chocolate Hershey's Hot Cocoa
directions: Mix sugar, cocoa and salt in large mug.
Heat milk in microwave at HIGH(100%) for 1 to 1-1/2 minutes or until hot.
Gradually add hot milk to cocoa mixture in mug, stirring until well blended.
Stir in vanilla.
VARIATIONS:
Rich and Adult: Increase cocoa to 2 tablespoons; follow recipe.
Lower Fat: Use nonfat milk; follow recipe.
Sugar Free: Omit sugar. Combine cocoa and salt. Proceed as above, using nonfat milk. Stir in vanilla and sugar substitute with sweetening of 2 tablespoons sugar.

name: Mexican Spiced Coffee
directions: COMBINE espresso, cinnamon, and nutmeg in coffee filter in an electric coffee maker.
POUR water into the water receptacle.
ADD SPLENDA® No Calorie Sweetener, Granular and orange juice to empty coffee pot; brew.
Serve immediately; black or with milk and additional SPLENDA® No Calorie Sweetener or Granular if desired.

name: Cafe Mocha Drink Mix
directions: Sift all ingredients together; Store in a nice 60 oz container.
To use: add 4 tbsp of mix to 8 oz of boiling/hot water.
Note: to the noob who left the one star review -- I know who you are so please stop stalking me with a new ID and grow up, huh?

name: Sangria
directions: Mix all ingredients except for ice.
Pour sangria in glass and then add ice and fruit.

name: Screwdriver Shooter
directions: In a 45ml (whiskey) shot glass combine vodka & Cointreau.
Drink.
ENJOY!

name: Margarita Shooter
directions: In a 45ml (whiskey) shot glass combine the ingredients.
Drink.
Enjoy.
Ask bartender for another.

name: Southern Sweet Iced Tea
directions: In a medium size pan fill 1/2 way with water.
add tea bags.
cook on medium heat until comes to a good boil. it will be foamy.
put sugar in gallon jug (pitcher).
pour boiling tea over sugar.
stir well to dissolve sugar.
add water until jug is full (may not use whole gallon of water).
stir well.
pour over ice (i like mine anyway i can get it ice or no ice).
drink and enjoy!
I USE RED DIAMOND OR LUZIANN TEA BAGS.

similar.name: Southern Sweet Iced Tea, Hotel Style

name: Kamikaze Shooter / Shot
directions: In a 45ml whiskey shot glass.
layer your ingredients.
serve.

name: Hot Milk (Alcoholic)
directions: Heat the milk in a saucepan over medium heat.
Pour into mugs and add Frangelico and rum, and stir with a spoon.
Sprinkle drink with cinnamon and nutmeg to taste.

name: Orgasm Shot/Shooter
directions: Pour in the Cointreau.
Pour in the Baileys slow & careful so it sits on the top.
Open up & say ahhhh.

name: Le Cap - Ferrat Cocktail
directions: Build the ingredients in a high-ball glass over ice.
top with soda water.
garnish with a wedge of lime or slice of orange.

name: Absolute Lush Cocktail
directions: Shake with ice, the vodka, Chambord, peach schnapps & apple juice.
Strain into a high-ball glass.
Top off with the lemon squash.

name: Light and Creamy Low Fat and Sugar Free Eggnog
directions: Place all of the ingredients in a blender, and blend for 30-60 seconds, or until smooth. Chill for several hours.
Shake or stir eggnog well to blend before serving. Serve in glasses or mugs with an additional sprinkle of nutmeg.

NOTES:  You can use 6 packets of Equal instead of the Splenda Granular.  If you'd rather not use artificial sweeteners, then substitute the Splenda with 1/4 cup sugar, and use 1/2 cup regular instant vanilla pudding instead of the 3 Tbsps sugar-free pudding.

name: Coffee Floats
directions: In a tall glass, mix together the chocolate syrup and the Kahlua.
Add enough seltzer water to fill the glass 2/3 full and stir well.
Add a scoop of coffee ice cream and serve.

name: Flavored Long Island Iced Tea
directions: Add ice to a cocktail shaker.
Add all ingredients.
Squeeze lemon juice into shaker and drop in lemon.
Close shaker and shake well.
Pour drink into large glass (pint beer glasses work well) and enjoy!

similar.name: Long Island Iced Tea (Applebee's/TGI Friday's style)

name: Sinless Dark Chocolate Hot Chocolate
directions: In a mug, combine the cocoa powder, sugar, and water.
Heat the milk and the cinnamon stick until slightly bubbling.
Remove the cinnamon stick and add the milk to the mug.
Combine and add the vanilla extract and slight dash of chili.
Top with mini marshmallows.

similar.name: Hot Cocoa Mix in a Mug

name: Café French (Canada)
directions: Combine the 1st 4 ingredients in a coffee mug & garnish w/whipped cream.

name: Italian "Coffee"
directions: Combine in coffee mug, garnish w/coriander & serve.

name: Hot Milk Posset
directions: Heat in a saucepan the milk, lemon peel, sugar and almond extract.
When it begins to scald, remove from heat.
Beat egg white and add.
Now add rum and brandy.
Serve hot.

name: Canadian Coffee
directions: For topping Whip cream with maple syrup just up until soft mounds; set aside.
FOr coffee Divide maple syrup and whiskey among 4 warmed heat proof glass mugs or goblets. Pour in coffee to 1 inch of top; spoon topping over coffee.
Serve!

name: Rachel's Mocha Loca Iced Coffee
directions: Mix hot water, instant coffee mix, sugar, and chocolate syrup in a small mug until all ingredients have dissolved.
Put 1/2 to 1 cup ice (depending on how cold you want it) in a tall mug or glass.
Pour the contents of the small mug over the ice.
Then stir in the milk.
That's it. It's that simple. Enjoy!

name: Iced Red Tea
directions: Pour 4 cups boiling water over tea bags, cover and steep 5 minutes. Remove tea bags from water, squeezing gently. Add 2 cups of cold water, and 1/2 cup sugar or Splenda, and stir until sugar dissolves. Serve over ice.

similar.name: Easy Iced Tea Otherwise Referred to As Fas-T

name: Dad's Iced Tea
directions: Warm your teapot or glass or ceramic saucepan by rinsing it with boiling water. Place Place tea bag(s) in pot. Pour boling water over bags. Cover and steep 3-5 minutes. Remove tea bags, add sugar, stir until sugar dissolves. Stir in 2 cups of water.

name: Fourth of July (Adult-Alcoholic)
directions: In clear glass layer is order given.
Do not stir until ready to drink.
Can be made in any amount just use equal amount of each liquid in each glass.

name: Melon Margarita
directions: combine the ice, melon liqueur, tequila, lemon juice & sugar syrup in a blender.
Blend until the mix looks like shaved ice.
Pour into a salt-rimmed cocktail glass.
Garnish with the lemon twist.

name: Snow-Capped Cinnamon Hot Cocoa
directions: COMBINE milk and syrup in 1-quart microwave-safe bowl.
Microwave on HIGH (100%) power for 5 minutes or until hot.
Stir in ground cinnamon.
Pour into 4 large mugs; garnish with whipped cream, morsels and cinnamon sticks.

similar.name: Instant Hot Cocoa With a Kick!

name: Almond Liqueur ( Amaretto)
directions: Combine first 3 ingredients in a Dutch oven; bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer 5 minutes, stirring occasionaly; cool completely. Stir in remaining ingredients; store in airtight containers( I use empty liquor bottles).

name: Spiced Coffee
directions: Place whole coffee beans in grinder; process to a medium grind.
Place coffee beans in coffee filter in coffee maker, sprinkle with nutmeg and flavorings.
Add water to coffee maker and brew.  Serve immediately with cream and sugar to taste.

name: Decadent Eggnog (Bailey's)
directions: In a lrg bowl, beat eggs till very thick & creamy. Gradually beat in sugar. Blend in milk & Bailey's.
Whip cream in another bowl till it holds soft peaks. Stir whipped cream into egg mixture.
Chill till ready to serve & stir again just before serving. (I like to dust the top w/nutmeg).

NOTE: Prep steps were repeated exactly as shown on the net. I skipped Step 2, added the unbeaten cream w/the milk + Bailey's & cannot imagine it being any better if done as written, but you may choose your method.

similar.name: Bailey's Irish Cream Liqueur (Gift-Giving or for Yourself!)

name: Hazel
directions: Place all ingredients into shaker with ice and strain into a martini glass.
Garnish with crushed hazelnuts.

name: Mrs. Bush's Hot Chocolate
directions: In a medium sized pot- mix cocoa, salt, and sugar.
Add milk. Heat to dissolve.
Add light cream, cinnamon, vanilla. Heat to just under boiling.
Mix very well and pour into warm mug.
Top with whipped cream, cocoa powder, and fine orange zest.

name: Coffee Eggnog
directions: Combine eggnog, ice cream, coffee powder, and rum extract in blender. Blend well.
Pour into mugs or glasses.
Top with whipped cream and nutmeg.

name: Medicinal  Cranberry Tea
directions: Bring one pint of water to a rolling boil. Insert your tea bag and steep for 3 to 6 minutes.
Pour into a one quart container.
Add two cups of  unsweetened Cranberry Juice.
Store in the refrigerator and use within three days.

name: Bloody Mary
directions: Whisk together first 5 ingredients in a large pitcher until well belnded. Place ice cubes in 8 old fashioned glasses, fill 2/3 full. Pour mixture evenly into glasses. Garnish, if desired.

name: White Hot Chocolate
directions: In a saucepan combine 1/4 C of cream, vanilla chips, cinnamon stick, and nutmeg.  Stir over low heat until chips melt completely.
Discard the cinnamon stick.
Add remaining cream, stir until heated through.
Remove from heat, add extracts.
Sprinkle each serving with ground cinnamon (optional) and serve.
Enjoy!

name: Black Duck Beachcomber
directions: Blend together in mixing glass and serve over ice.

name: Big Coffee Chill
directions: In blender combine first 6 ingredients.
Blend few seconds.
Add 8 ice cubes and blend.
Add remaining 8 ice cubes and blend again until smooth.

name: Black Duck Martini
directions: Mix tigether and pour over ice.
Garnish with lime wedge.

name: My Guilt Free Caramel Hot Chocolate
directions: Boil water.
Add all ingredients and stir.
Enjoy.

name: Energy Herbal Tea
directions: Add to infuser and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Sweeten with honey if desired.

name: Licorice Mint Iced Tea
directions: In a large pot bring water to a boil over high heat. Stir in the licorice and cinnamon; lower to a simmer, partially cover the pot, and simmer for 10 minutes. Remove from heat, stir in peppermint, cover and let steep for 10 minutes.
Place a large strainer lined with cheesecloth or damp paper towel over another pot or heat-proof bowl. Strain tea. Stir in honey until dissolved and let cool completely, about 1 hour. Serve over ice.

name: Hibiscus Cocktail
directions: Fill a cocktail shaker with ice.
Add tea and tequila, cover, and shake to combine.
Serve immediately.

name: Deluxe Cocoa Mix
directions: Mix all the ingredients together and put in a large container with a lid.
To make a single cup of cocoa: 1/3 cup of mix stirred with 3/4 cup of boiling water until chips begin to melt.  Just adjust these measurements according to size of your cup.

similar.name: Quick and Easy, Chocolaty & Creamy, Hot Cocoa
Hot Cocoa Mix in a Mug

name: Sugar Free Hot Chocolate
directions: Mix Splenda and cocoa in small saucepan.
Gradually add milk, whisking until blended.
Cook over medium-low heat until hot, but do not boil.
Serve in your favorite mug.
Squirt the top with a small amount of Reddi-Wip if desired and sprinkle with cinnamon.
For mint flavored, try adding a DROP of mint extract tho the milk before simmering.
For mocha flavored, add 2 teaspoons of instant coffee to the milk before simmering.
There are endless possibilities to this yummy drink!

name: Chambord Brunch Cocktail
directions: Add Chambord & pineapple juice to a champagne flute.
Top with champagne.

name: Almond Cream Liqueur
directions: Pour everything into a blender and blend until smooth.
Store in an airtight container in refrigerator.
Shake well before serving.
Serve chilled.

name: Delicious Irish Cream (No Eggs)
directions: In a blender combine all ingredients; blend on high power for about 20-30 seconds.
Store tightly sealed in the refrigerator.
Shake well before using.

similar.name: Bailey's Irish Cream Liqueur (Gift-Giving or for Yourself!)

name: Creamy Chocolate Almond Coffee
directions: In a small, chilled mixing bowl, beat whipping cream, powdered sugar, and almond extract with an electric mixer on high speed until stiff peaks form.
Place 1 large scoop (1/2 cup)ice cream in each of 4 large coffee mugs.
Pour coffee over ice cream.
Top with whipped cream, and garnish with shaved chocolate and toasted almond slices.

name: Hot Spiced Cranberry Cider
directions: In a large pot, combine apple cider, cranberry juice, brown sugar, cinnamon sticks, cloves and lemon slices.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 15 to 20 minutes.  That's it!  You're done.

(Juniperwoman's Note: If you like, you can use a slotted spoon to remove cinnamon, cloves, and lemon slices, but we just left them in because they looked so festive!   If you do remove the lemon slices, you could float individual slices in each serving cup.).

name: Creamy Hot Chocolate Mix in a Jar (For Gift-Giving)
directions: In a large bowl combine all ingredients together; mix very well to combine.
Place into clean glass jars; seal tightly.
Decorate as desired.
The mixture will keep well tightly sealed for up to 3 months.

similar.name: Candy Cane Hot Cocoa Mix - Gift

name: Hot Chocolate
directions: Make a paste with the chocolate and milk. If the paste starts to get too dry, add more milk.
When most or all of the chocolate is dissolved, add the remaining milk and pour into a sauce pan.
Add the sugar.
Heat over low to medium heat while stirring constantly until it steams.
Pour into your favourite mug and enjoy!

similar.name: Hot Chocolate With Skim Milk, Cocoa Powder and Maple Syrup
My Guilt Free Caramel Hot Chocolate

name: Mediterranean Coffee
directions: Place coffee, sugar, chocolate syrup, anise seed, cloves and cinnamon into a sauce pan
Heat to 200 F degrees over medium heat
Strain into mugs
Top with whipped cream and twists.

name: Peppermint-Mocha Coffee
directions: Pour coffee into large saucepan.
Add chocolate; cook on low heat 5 minute or until chocolate is melted, stirring occasionally.
Add milk and extract; stir until well blended.
Garnish each serving with a peppermint stick.

name: Hot Christmas Tea
directions: In saucepan, bring water to boil. Add tea and spices, cover, and let stand 5 minutes.
Remove tea bags.
Stir in sugar, cranberry juice, and wine.
Return to medium heat and stir until sugar is dissolved and mixture is heated through.
Serve in cups with fruit slices as garnish.

name: Orange Cafe Au Lait Mix- Gift in a Jar
directions: Place all ingredients in food processor.
Cover and process 5 to 10 seconds, until well mixed.
Store in tightly covered container at room temperature up to 6 months.
For each serving, place 2 teaspoons mix in cup. Fill with 2/3 cup boiling water.

similar.name: Low-Cal Iced Coffee With Milk (Cafe Au Lait)
Jenn's Sugar Free Dark Chocolate Orange Faux Chai Latte Coffee

name: Low Carb Kahlua Liqueur
directions: Combine the water, splenda, vanilla and coffee in a saucepan and heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally until the splenda and coffee are dissolved.
Remove from the heat and add the vodka.
Cover and let cool.
Store in an empty bottle with a screw top/resealable lid.
Store in a cool dark place - best if stored about 30 days before drinking.

name: Homemade Eggnog
directions: In large pot, heat up all ingredients except whiskey and eggs.
Separate eggs.
Return yellows to milk mixture.
Beat egg whites with a little sugar to form peak.
Return to milk mixture.
Add whiskey to taste.

name: Eggnog - Light
directions: *Place all of the ingredients in a blender, and blend for 30-60 seconds, or until smooth. *Chill for several hours.
*Shake or stir eggnog well to blend before serving.
*Serve in glasses or mugs with an additional sprinkle of nutmeg.

name: Hot Vanilla
directions: Heat milk to just below boiling point, approximately 1 1/2 minutes in microwave safe measuring cup.
Stir in splenda and vanilla.
Garnish with fat free whipped topping and cinnamon if desired.
Serve immediately.

name: Amaretto Coffee Creamer
directions: In a screw-top lid jar, place all ingredients and shake well to blend.
Add 1-2 tablespoons of creamer to 6 ounces of coffee. Enjoy!

name: Cappuccino Coffee Creamer
directions: Place all ingredients in a jar with a screw-top lid. Shake until well combined.
Add 1-2 tablespoons of creamer to 6 ounces of coffee. Enjoy!

name: Mocha Coffee Creamer
directions: Place all ingredients in a jar with a screw-top lid.
Shake well to combine.
Add 1-2 tablespoons to 6 ounces of coffee. Enjoy!

name: Café L'orange
directions: In a small bowl, combine all ingredients until well blended.
To hot water in a mug, add 1 or 2 teaspoons of mix and stir.
Store mix in air tight container,.

name: Hot-Hot Chocolate Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients well and store in airtight container. (Mix is good for 3 months).
To make hot chocolate: Mix 1 cup of hot water with 1/2 cup of hot chocolate mix.
Stir well & enjoy!
Note: The hot chocolate is GREAT with a little whipped cream on top and a sprinkle of cinnamon & cayenne pepper.

similar.name: Instant Hot Cocoa With a Kick!

name: Another Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: Mix all ingredients together in a large bowl until blended (you can mix in blender or food processor for a finer texture if desired).
Store in air tight container.
To serve: Mix 6 tablespoons mix with 1 cup hot water until mix is dezolved. Top with marshmallows if desired.

name: Decadent White Hot Chocolate
directions: Heat all ingredients in a saucepan over medium hit, stirring constantly with whisk, until vanilla chips melt and heated to your liking.  Be careful not to scorch the milk!
Sprinkle with ground cinnamon (optional).

name: Hot Chocolate Eggnog
directions: Pour Eggnog and Milk into the cup.
Whisk until mixed throughly.
Place in Microwave for 1:15-1:30 minute.
Whisk in the Packet of Hot Cocoa Mix.
Enjoy!
OPTIONAL: Top with whipped cream.

name: Spice Tea
directions: add ingredients together, shake it up abit, and thats it! EASY!
for one cup of hot water add 2-3 teaspoons more or less depending on how you like it. me and my husband love it strong.

name: Homemade Instant Suisse Mocha Mix
directions: Stir ingredients together.
Process in a blender until powdered.
Store in an airtight container.
Attach a note to the container: "Use 1 cup of hot water and 2 tablespoons of coffee mix for each cup of coffee.".

similar.name: Lovely and Luscious Mug of Coffee

name: Sugar Free Mocha Hot Chocolate
directions: Mix together SPLENDA® No Calorie Sweetener and cocoa in a small saucepan.
Gradually add milk, whisking until blended.
Cook over medium-low heat until thoroughly heated. Serve immediately.

similar.name: Coffee Slushie, Low Sugar  -  Fat Free

name: Lower Calorie Eggnog
directions: Mix together first 3 ingredients in a large heavy saucepan.
Set aside.
Whisk egg yolks.
Add to SPLENDA® Granular mixture whisking until blended.
Gradually whisk in milk.
Cook over low heat, whisking constantly until the temperature reaches 175 degrees approximately (5 to 8 minutes).
Remove from heat and whisk in half and half; cool.
Cover and chill 3 hours or up to 3 days. Stir in vanilla extract just before serving.
Their website also says you that if desired, 2 tablespoons of vanilla extract can be replaced with 1 cup dark rum.

similar.name: Light and Creamy Low Fat and Sugar Free Eggnog

name: Hot Chocolate With Peppermint Schnapps
directions: Heat 1 cup of the milk in a medium saucepan over low heat.
Add chocolate, stirring constantly, until melted.
When the chocolate has melted, increase heat to medium and add the remainder of the milk while whisking rapidly. Do not allow to boil.
